_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Design and develop cybersecurity or cybersecurity-enabled products.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to design security and privacy architectures that enable data utility and AI innovation while maintaining protection levels appropriate to the data sensitivity and regulatory context.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-A-003 (SP, cosine: 0.468), SP-T-001 (SP, cosine: 0.464).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.0/10 (raw 0.701). Shared vocabulary: [design]. DCWF-only terms [cybersecurity, develop, enabled, products] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, ai, appropriate, architectures, context, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.862 (P=0.893, R=0.834). P exceeds R by 0.059 — the DCWF element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 26 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.2%). "Design and develop cybersecurity or cybersecurity-enabled products." and "Ability to design security and privacy architectures that enable data utility an" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.497 < STS 0.701 (gap -0.203). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [cybersecurity, develop, enabled] and [ability, ai, appropriate] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.4%. Shared tokens: [design]. DCWF-only: [cybersecurity, develop, enabled, products]. WIDAI-only: [ability, ai, appropriate, architectures, context, data].
